blocks.speaker: Cosine Silver
blocks.content: Aha! So you're an educator. Pleased to meet you. I'm Cosine Silver, fourth-rank noble. My father serves as Creas's magistrate. Forgive my boldness, but I fell for you at first sight. If you'd grant me the honor of escorting you home, I'd be eternally grateful.
Shiira wriggled upright.
But instead of looking at Silver, she held her forehead and sighed.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Ugh, annoying. I just wanted to get drunk today.
Nothing seemed to go right today. Showing weakness in front of her students, being dismissed by Keira, and now strange men pestering her—wasn't it too much?
A bitter smile crossed Shiira's lips. Maybe she looked exactly like a heartbroken woman right now.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Heh. At least someone likes me. A temperamental, unkind woman like me.

She'd never once felt love for anyone. When Armin appeared with Keira three years ago, she hadn't felt betrayed. But perhaps there'd been a sliver of hope—a faint thought of what if they met again. Could that even be called love?

blocks.speaker: Cosine Silver
blocks.content: My introduction was too grandiose. My apologies. Meeting a woman I can't let slip made me eager to impress. Forgive my rudeness.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: It's fine. I'm tired, so please leave.
Shiira was exhausted. Though a certified 6th-class mage could sober up easily, she still needed the alcohol's dreamy haze.

blocks.speaker: Cosine Silver
blocks.content: Leaving a lady alone goes against chivalry. Let me escort you by carriage. Lean on me.
As Silver grabbed Shiira's shoulder to help her up, Shirone's group immediately protested.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What are you doing? Don't touch Teacher.

blocks.speaker: Cosine Silver
blocks.content: Seems there's a misunderstanding. I'm just offering an escort. Kids shouldn't interfere. The teacher can take the carriage, and you can go back.
Silver tried to force Shiira up. Shirone grabbed his wrist.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Teacher said no. Please leave. We'll take care of her.

blocks.speaker: Cosine Silver
blocks.content: Damn brats getting cocky. Earlier, I saw you getting her drunk—what's your game? Which school are you from? Want to get expelled?
Shirone's expression turned cold. Bringing up school was clearly pressure—a teacher causing a scene here would invite gossip.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: So you've been watching us all along. Then it's even less your place to interfere. And let me add—the teacher here is beyond your reach.

blocks.speaker: Cosine Silver
blocks.content: You? You dare use 'you' with me?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Should I call you something else? Pervert, perhaps?

blocks.speaker: Cosine Silver
blocks.content: You little—!
Silver's drunkenness turned to rage as he grabbed Iruki's collar.
But a sharp pain in his side made him release his grip. Clutching his stomach, Silver staggered back.

blocks.speaker: Cosine Silver
blocks.content: Guh! What the—?
Nade generated electricity in his palm.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Scram. Next time, I'll fry your lower half.
The burly man, who'd been ready to intervene, stepped forward with a serious expression.

blocks.speaker: Burly man
blocks.content: Hey, they're magic school students.

blocks.speaker: Cosine Silver
blocks.content: Magic school? Alpheas Magic School?
Silver's face paled. Magic schools were on a different level from regular noble schools. And Alpheas was one of the kingdom's top five prestigious institutions, attended only by the highest nobles.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Heard you'll get us expelled?

blocks.speaker: Cosine Silver
blocks.content: No, that's…

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: I'm Mercodain Iruki. Expel me if you can.

blocks.speaker: Cosine Silver
blocks.content: M-Mercodain?
The alcohol seemed to evaporate from his system. No noble in Tormia Kingdom was unfamiliar with the name Mercodain.
A first-rank noble family, they had led the king's advisory council, 'Dragon Thunder,' for centuries without interruption.
As Silver's pupils shook, Iruki smirked, unsurprised.

blocks.speaker: Burly man
blocks.content: Let's go, Silver. Hey, Silver!
The burly man dragged Silver away. Silver, grateful for the out, fled like a whipped dog.
Even after they left, Shirone's group seethed. Their blatant disrespect proved they hadn't taken the students seriously—especially with their small statures.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This place has terrible vibes. Never judge by appearances. Let's never come back.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Agreed. Let's just leave. It's getting dark.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Teacher. Are you okay?

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Yeah. Let's go.
With Nade's help, Shiira stood unsteadily.
The unwanted attention had irritated her, but she wasn't in a bad mood. With her students protecting her, what was there to fear?
Even a certified 6th-class mage was just a woman who needed escorting under noble etiquette outside.
She found her students oddly endearing.
After paying the bill without fuss, Shiira staggered out.
The moon hung in the sky.
Under the cloudless night, bathed in moonlight, Shiira let out a euphoric sigh.
She felt inexplicably happy. Maybe it was the alcohol, or perhaps she'd taken a new leap today. For the first time, she felt free from Armin's shadow, ready to live her own life.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Ahh, I feel great. So great today.
Like a girl, Shiira spun under the moon.
Shirone's group, just exiting the restaurant, gaped.
Had the alcohol briefly melted her frozen heart?
Her purple hair cascading loose, she was beautiful under the moonlight. Her twirling trench coat was amusing too.
Passersby glanced at her, but none frowned or clicked their tongues.
She was that lovable.
Shirone's group didn't stop her. Instead, they watched her drunken antics with fascination.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Teacher's like this? Unexpected.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Did we… just hit the jackpot?
Iruki's eyes gleamed with an idea.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Hmm. Could this be used as leverage? To stop the research club's dissolution.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: If we play it right, maybe. Teacher isn't the type to lie. She'd probably be too embarrassed to remember anyway.
As Nade and Iruki schemed, Shirone pointed and shouted.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hey! There!
Shiira, spinning in place, stumbled.
Like a top losing momentum, she was about to fall.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Grab her!
Letting her fall in public would be disastrous.
Shirone caught her back while Nade and Iruki held her arms.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Teacher, snap out of it. We need you sober to get back to school.

blocks.speaker: Shiira
blocks.content: Ugh. Dizzy.
Shiira staggered in zigzags. Downing a bottle of cognac in an hour and spinning dozens of times—no wonder she was out of it.
It was less drinking and more punishment.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hey, hey! This isn't working. Shirone, try carrying her.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: What? Why me?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Out of us, you're the strongest.
At least Shirone, having grown up climbing mountains and chopping wood, was stronger than his friends.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Fine. Then help her onto my back.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Don't look so reluctant. How often do you get to carry Teacher? I'm jealous.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Tch. If you can't say it, don't.
Hoisting Shiira onto his back, Shirone started walking.
As they left the noble district, the crowd thinned.
For commoners living hand-to-mouth, leisure was a luxury.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hey… Teacher's surprisingly small. She seemed as tall as me, but she's tiny.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Probably psychological. Teachers always seem bigger.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Really? But she's heavier than I thought. It's getting hard.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Heh. Of course she's heavy. Women are made of bones and muscle too. Did you think she'd be light as a feather?
He hadn't thought that, but he'd vaguely expected her to be lighter.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No! I knew that much. But this is really tough. I might not make it to school.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: If you can't, neither can we. Just keep going.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Can't we use magic? Float her with air magic.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Even so, she's our teacher. That'd be…
Now that he pictured it, it'd look odd.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Three guys can't carry one girl? Pathetic. Now I miss Rian. He could've carried me while I carried Teacher. Should've learned Schema.
Nade and Iruki stayed silent. Honestly, failing to carry a petite woman was demoralizing.
But women were heavier than expected, and bookish types like them had no strength.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What can we do? The only exercise we get is breathing. I'm confident in mental tasks, though.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Then use your mind. Learn Schema right now. Shirone, think you can?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: You can try. I mastered Spirit Zone in one go. Don't tell me you couldn't?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, I got it on my first try too. Once I got the feel, it worked. Shirone, what about you?
Could someone enter Spirit Zone without knowing it? That was so like Shirone, Iruki giggled.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Interesting. Anyway, that's my point. The basics are the same, right?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Maybe? Should I really try? My arms are killing me.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Do it, do it. First, enter a trance and project your body's image. Then…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I know the theory. Let me focus.
As his friends fell silent, Shirone closed his eyes.
The trance-like state before entering Spirit Zone. From there, project the body's image and awaken the senses through insight!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ugh… Ugh! No, it's getting worse.
Iruki stifled a laugh.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Idiot, did you really think it'd work? Excelling at Spirit Zone means you're hopeless at Schema.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Pfft—hahaha!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Screw this! I quit! Switch!
They took turns carrying Shiira.
But Nade collapsed after half the distance Shirone had covered, and Iruki couldn't manage ten steps.
Shirone, hoisting Shiira again with trembling arms, was exasperated.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't we look pathetic?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Some thoughts shouldn't be spoken aloud, Shirone.
With Nade's words, the group fell silent. Not out of shame—they were just too tired to talk.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hey… let's drop that.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Drop what?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Using today to negotiate with Teacher.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Hmm.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: But the research club's survival is at stake.
